title: hormathophylla lapeyrouseana subsp. angustifolia
intro: Hormathophylla lapeyrouseana subsp. angustifolia, also known as the Corsican cabbage or prickly cabbage, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Mediterranean region. It possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb for medicinal purposes. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system. Hormathophylla lapeyrouseana subsp. angustifolia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability and use may be limited due to its rarity.
